subject: 📊 Daily Portfolio: Markets Closed (Sat Jul 12) | ANET exit likely ✓ · CELC PDUFA Fri Jul 17

🔔 Action Items

🔔 ANET — LIMIT SELL AT $185 ALMOST CERTAINLY TRIGGERED

Jul 9 intraday high: ~$189.82  |  Jul 9 close: ~$184.69–$185.03
Jul 10 close: ~$186.96  |  Your GTC limit: $185.00
ANET traded above $185 on both July 9 and July 10. If your limit order was GTC, it almost certainly filled Wednesday or Thursday.

► Open IBKR Monday morning and confirm the fill. Plan executed as designed.

Gross P&L (1.27 × ($185.00 − $157.7874)):+$34.56
Exit commission:−$3.00
Net P&L:+$31.56  (+15.8%)

If filled: estimated cash balance becomes ~$497.36 ($265.41 original + $231.95 net proceeds). Original exit plan honored. Do NOT re-enter ANET before Q2 earnings print (~Aug 4).

✓ VERA — FDA APPROVED (Jul 7) · HOLDING

FDA granted accelerated approval for Trutakna™ (atacicept-vymj) on Monday July 7, 2026 for IgA Nephropathy.
Last known close (Jul 10): ~$41.82 vs. your avg entry $41.7755 — essentially flat. Normal post-approval drift after the +8% spike on approval day. Thesis remains intact.
14 analysts · Strong Buy · avg target $78 (BofA $70, Guggenheim $76, TD Cowen $78).

► Next binary: ORIGIN 3 confirmatory eGFR endpoint — Q3 2026.

Accelerated approval is conditional on ORIGIN 3. If the confirmatory eGFR endpoint misses, FDA can withdraw approval. Phase 3 proteinuria data (−46% vs placebo) is strong — most analysts view the confirmatory risk as manageable. WAC $32,700/28-day cycle sets a high commercial ceiling if utilization builds. The stock sitting near your entry after a +8% approval pop then fading is normal — institutions are sizing in slowly, not all at once.

Stop (pre-PDUFA floor, still valid):$38.00   ~9.1% below ~$41.82 — safe, no action
TP1 — sell 4 shares at:$55.00 · post-approval stabilization zone
TP2 — sell remaining 4 at:$78.00 · analyst consensus midpoint (BofA $70 → $78 ladder)

⏳ Pending Watchlist

CAPR  Capricor Therapeutics  ❌ HALAL FAIL — DO NOT ENTER

Zoya screening Jul 7 2026: interest income $2.2M / revenue $22.3M = 9.9% — exceeds AAOIFI 5% cap. Non-compliant.

AdCom: Wednesday July 29 — 17 days away 🏹 (CTGTAC panel reviews BLA for deramiocel, DMD cardiomyopathy)
PDUFA: Friday August 22, 2026 — CRL lifted, Class 2 resubmission, Phase 3 HOPE-3 primary endpoint met (PUL v2.0 + LVEF)

This is an awareness marker only. Halal fail overrides all setup quality. No entry under any circumstances.

VRDN — 📁 ARCHIVED. FDA approved Lumvoa (veligrotug) June 30, 2026. Entry missed (IBKR blocker). No further action needed.

VERA (watchlist) — 📁 ARCHIVED. Position entered June 29, 2026. Tracked via positions[]. Clean up via portfolio-manager.

📡 Catalyst Plays — Saturday July 12, 2026

15-search sweep completed. 3 names surfaced. CELC fails market-cap gate (large-cap awareness). TENX passes size gate, date borderline (47 days). CAPR halal fail confirmed (same as watchlist).

LARGE-CAP · AWARENESS ONLY — fails <$2B gate  🔴 PDUFA IN 5 DAYS

🔮 CELC — Celcuity, Inc.

Gedatolisib (pan-PI3K/mTOR) + fulvestrant · VIKTORIA-1 · HR+/HER2−/PIK3CA-WT advanced breast cancer (2nd line)

Catalyst:PDUFA Friday July 17 — 5 days 🔴
Market cap:~$5.6B — fails <$2B gate. Awareness only.
Phase 3 (VIKTORIA-1):PFS 9.3 vs 2.0 mo — strong clinical signal; Priority Review + RTOR
Approval probability est.:~80–85% (RTOR + Priority Review + robust PFS)
Indication size:~37,000 PIK3CA-WT patients/yr in US · >$5B TAM
Halal:⚠️ Unverified (pre-revenue biotech — check Musaffa)
Short interest:~22% of float

Why it matters to your portfolio: A CELC approval on Friday could lift the broader biotech sector and drag VERA higher in sympathy. Watch VERA’s Monday open. Say ‘analyze CELC’ for full deep dive.

🏹 WATCH · Pre-score 2/5 · Small-cap ✓ · Date: 47 days (borderline)

🔮 TENX — Tenax Therapeutics, Inc.

TNX-103 (oral levosimendan) · Phase 3 LEVEL trial · Pulmonary Hypertension in HFpEF (PH-HFpEF)

Catalyst:Phase 3 topline data at ESC Congress — August 28–31 (Munich) — ~47 days 🏹
Market cap:~$470M ✓ passes <$2B gate
Enrollment:230+ patients randomized (completed Q1 2026) · data locked
Bull scenario:Primary endpoint met → first-ever oral therapy for PH-HFpEF; large underserved market
Bear scenario:Endpoint miss → typical small-cap cardiac failure −40–60% in one session
Cash position:Net loss $15.7M Q1 2026 · no revenue — check runway before entry
Halal:⚠️ Unverified — cardiovascular biotech, no obvious haram revenue. Check Musaffa.
Smart money:Insider buying signals management confidence per sell-side notes. No 13F cluster confirmed.

Pre-score 2/5 — conviction needs halal clearance before escalating to WATCH-active

Verdict: WATCH. Date is 47 days, slightly outside the 45-day screen. Re-check in ~2 weeks as ESC approaches. Screen halal first. Position cap if entered: 30% of portfolio (~$250). Say ‘analyze TENX’ for full deep dive.

❌ SKIP · Halal Fail

CAPR — Capricor Therapeutics

AdCom July 29 · PDUFA August 22 · deramiocel for DMD cardiomyopathy

❌ Halal fail: Zoya 9.9% interest income/revenue (AAOIFI threshold 5%). Shown for completeness — do not enter regardless of setup quality or AdCom outcome.

💵 Cash Deployment

On record: $265.41 (verify in IBKR — connector offline).

If ANET limit filled at $185 (very likely):
Net proceeds: 1.27 × $185 − $3.00 commission = +$231.95
Estimated total cash: $265.41 + $231.95 = ~$497
Minimum cash reserve: $3 (to cover exit commission) → Deployable: ~$491
30% portfolio cap (on ~$832 total): ~$250 max for any single new position

Recommendation: Hold dry powder until Monday IBKR confirmation + halal screens run.

Option A — TENX (WATCH): If Musaffa clears TENX halal status, consider a starter position (~$125–$150, ~1/4 of max) in 2–3 weeks as ESC data approaches. Binary cardiac readout — size conservatively. Do not enter without halal clearance.

Option B — Add to VERA: VERA risk score: HIGH (post-accelerated approval, ORIGIN 3 pending). Rules say no adding to HIGH risk with idle cash. Not recommended.

Option C — Hold as dry powder: CELC PDUFA Friday (Jul 17) may lift biotech broadly — watch VERA for sympathy move and potential TP1 setup near $55. Keep powder dry for better-screened catalyst plays.

Recommended: Option C — dry powder. Your call.

📊 Position Risk Scores (manual — IBKR offline)

Ticker Score Label Key Driver
VERA 58/100 HIGH Post-accelerated approval; ORIGIN 3 confirmatory trial pending Q3 2026. FDA can withdraw on miss. No revenue yet. Stop at $38 mitigates downside.
ANET* 23/100 LOW Strong FCF, AI Ethernet leadership, Q1 rev +35.1% YoY. Low risk — but likely exited (*confirm fill in IBKR Monday).

📚 Day 11 — Hyperscaler Capex

Hyperscalers are the four cloud giants: Microsoft, Amazon (AWS), Alphabet (Google Cloud), Meta. They build the data centers everyone else rents. Their combined capex is the single biggest demand driver for anything AI-adjacent — NVDA for chips, AVGO for custom silicon, ANET for AI Ethernet fabric. Your ANET trade was a pure hyperscaler capex play: you bought in when AI networking demand was accelerating and sold into the TP. ANET guided Q2 2026 at $2.8B revenue — a direct read-through from Microsoft and Meta raising capex budgets.

2026 combined hyperscaler capex runs ~$725B, up from ~$410B in 2025 — a 77% surge. Every quarter, the four earnings calls (January, April, July, October) reset the tempo for AI-adjacent positions. The order of influence: META and MSFT lead; GOOGL and AMZN follow. Smaller players like Oracle and CoreWeave are starting to matter too. The phrase to watch for on FinTwit: “AI capex digestion” — that’s how the market signals the cycle is turning before the stocks move.

How to use this: When you next rotate into an AI networking or AI chip name, start by checking what META and MSFT said about capex in their most recent earnings call. If both are raising guidance, the thesis is intact. If either signals “capex plateau,” trim fast — the stocks front-run the fundamental by 2–3 months. You had ANET in exactly the right window: Q1 MSFT capex was strong → ANET beat and guided up → you sold into the rally. That’s the causal chain.

Also today: Capex intensity — capex as % of revenue. Rising = company investing for the future. Falling = harvesting existing install base. TSMC rising capex intensity (now ~40% of revenue) signals they’re building capacity ahead of demand — bullish read for the AI supply chain.

Research only — not financial advice. All decisions are Abbas Al Madani’s own.
Prices: Web-sourced, IBKR connector offline this run. VERA: ~$41.82 (web, Thu Jul 10 close). ANET: ~$186.96 (web, Thu Jul 10 close). Neither IBKR-verified.
